title: [InstCombine] Replace an integer comparison of a `phi` node with multiple `ucmp`/`scmp` operands and a constant with `phi` of individual comparisons of original intrinsic's arguments

tests.source_program:
define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb1:
  %cmp1 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br label %exit

bb2:
  %cmp2 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %y, i16 %z)
  br label %exit

bb3:
  %cmp3 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %z, i16 %x)
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp1, %bb1 ], [ %cmp2, %bb2 ], [ %cmp3, %bb3 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

declare i8 @llvm.scmp(i16, i16)
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb1:
  %cmp1 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  %r1 = icmp slt i8 %cmp1, 0
  br label %exit

bb2:
  %cmp2 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %y, i16 %z)
  %r2 = icmp slt i8 %cmp2, 0
  br label %exit

bb3:
  %cmp3 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %z, i16 %x)
  %r3 = icmp slt i8 %cmp3, 0
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i1 [ %r1, %bb1 ], [ %r2, %bb2 ], [ %r3, %bb3 ]
  ret i1 %phi
}

declare i8 @llvm.scmp(i16, i16)
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary example only demonstrates 2 phi operands, but this test has 3 predecessors feeding the phi node. The summary mentions 'multiple edges' support but doesn't explicitly cover 3+ operand cases.

alive2.src:
define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb1:
  %cmp1 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br label %exit

bb2:
  %cmp2 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %y, i16 %z)
  br label %exit

bb3:
  %cmp3 = call i8 @llvm.scmp(i16 %z, i16 %x)
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp1, %bb1 ], [ %cmp2, %bb2 ], [ %cmp3, %bb3 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}

declare i8 @llvm.scmp(i16, i16)
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb1:                                              ; preds = %entry
  %0 = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  br label %exit

bb2:                                              ; preds = %check2
  %1 = icmp slt i16 %y, %z
  br label %exit

bb3:                                              ; preds = %check2
  %2 = icmp slt i16 %z, %x
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %bb3, %bb2, %bb1
  %phi = phi i1 [ %0, %bb1 ], [ %1, %bb2 ], [ %2, %bb3 ]
  ret i1 %phi
}

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb2:
  %cmp2 = scmp i8 i16 %y, %z
  %#range_0_%cmp2 = !range i8 %cmp2, i8 255, i8 2
  br label %exit

bb3:
  %cmp3 = scmp i8 i16 %z, %x
  %#range_1_%cmp3 = !range i8 %cmp3, i8 255, i8 2
  br label %exit

bb1:
  %cmp1 = scmp i8 i16 %x, %y
  %#range_2_%cmp1 = !range i8 %cmp1, i8 255, i8 2
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %#range_2_%cmp1, %bb1 ], [ %#range_0_%cmp2, %bb2 ], [ %#range_1_%cmp3, %bb3 ]
  %r = icmp slt i8 %phi, 0
  ret i1 %r
}
=>
define i1 @three_predecessor_phi_scmp(i1 %c1, i1 %c2, i16 %x, i16 %y, i16 %z) {
entry:
  br i1 %c1, label %bb1, label %check2

check2:
  br i1 %c2, label %bb2, label %bb3

bb2:
  %#0 = icmp slt i16 %y, %z
  br label %exit

bb3:
  %#1 = icmp slt i16 %z, %x
  br label %exit

bb1:
  %#2 = icmp slt i16 %x, %y
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i1 [ %#2, %bb1 ], [ %#0, %bb2 ], [ %#1, %bb3 ]
  ret i1 %phi
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i1 @ucmp_phi_with_ult_predicate(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %true, label %false

true:
  %cmp1 = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  br label %exit

false:
  %cmp2 = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %y, i16 %x)
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i8 [ %cmp1, %true ], [ %cmp2, %false ]
  %r = icmp ult i8 %phi, 1
  ret i1 %r
}

declare i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16, i16)
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @ucmp_phi_with_ult_predicate(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:
  br i1 %c, label %true, label %false

true:
  %cmp1 = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %x, i16 %y)
  %r1 = icmp ult i8 %cmp1, 1
  br label %exit

false:
  %cmp2 = call i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16 %y, i16 %x)
  %r2 = icmp ult i8 %cmp2, 1
  br label %exit

exit:
  %phi = phi i1 [ %r1, %true ], [ %r2, %false ]
  ret i1 %phi
}

declare i8 @llvm.ucmp(i16, i16)
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions ucmp as a CmpIntrinsic but the known test example only shows scmp. This tests unsigned comparison intrinsic with unsigned less-than predicate, which is not explicitly demonstrated.

alive2.src:

false:

exit:

; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @ucmp_phi_with_ult_predicate(i1 %c, i16 %x, i16 %y) {
entry:

true:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %0 = icmp eq i16 %x, %y
  br label %exit

false:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %1 = icmp eq i16 %y, %x
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %false, %true
  %phi = phi i1 [ %0, %true ], [ %1, %false ]
  ret i1 %phi
}

exit:

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't specify which icmp predicates are supported. The known test uses 'slt' but this tests 'eq' predicate, which is a different comparison operation not explicitly covered.

exit:
